mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns do not significantly exceed baseline when FEDFUNDS declines for three consecutive months (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5% within a single month, it signals a rapid deterioration in labor demand that triggers risk-off positioning, depressing S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above 4.5% in a single month
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.5%, or Rank IC remains above 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and consumer credit indicate a synchronized contraction in real economic activity and borrowing capacity, which compresses equity multiples over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are not significantly lower when both INDPRO and RSAFS decline simultaneously compared to periods where only one declines (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, accelerating housing starts amplify demand-pull pressures that force the Fed to maintain restrictive policy, thereby capping equity valuations over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: Monthly forward returns do not decline when HOUST rises alongside CPIAUCSL above 3.2, or the interaction term in a regression model is statistically insignificant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production reflects expanding capacity utilization and stronger corporate throughput, which directly supports earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive correlation with month-over-month INDPRO growth over any 2-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0%, the cost of capital becomes sufficiently restrictive to compress P/E multiples, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative when FEDFUNDS remains above 4.0% for consecutive months, or the signal hit rate falls below 45% over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising unemployment and stagnant non-farm payrolls indicates a labor market inflection point where wage growth decelerates, reducing inflation expectations and equity risk premiums, boosting returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns do not increase when UNRATE rises and PAYEMS growth decelerates simultaneously, or the cross-product term in a predictive regression lacks statistical significance (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During a post-shock recovery phase, accelerating real GDP growth drives strong earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: Monthly forward returns fail to outperform when GDPC1 growth accelerates, or the signal predictive power vanishes during high-volatility equity regimes (VIX > 25).
